cd:Float.cd:Description: Frequency separation of neighbouring looks.
Range look filter width.
Azimuth look filter width.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Number of individual looks.
Number of range pixels from input L1 product to be used in the estimation of the OSW. Only used for SM, for WV all range pixels are used.
Number of azimuth pixels from input L1 product to be used in the estimation of the OSW. Only used for SM, for WV all range pixels are used.
Number of azimuth pixels in cartesian co- and cross-spectra [pixels]: N_x=256
Number of range pixels in cartesian co- and cross-spectra [pixels]: N_y=256
Order of Hanning window function over the range axis: β_x=1
Order of Hanning window function over the azimuth axis: β_y=1
Width of low-pass filter for detrending of SLC image. The default value are: filerwidth=(500 ,500)
Specifies the periodogram size used to estimate the cross-spectra in TOPS mode, applied over the azimuth and range dimensions [Pixel]. Default value: (300,600 )
Specifies the maximum size, over azimuth and range dimension, of the estimation area form intra/inter-Burst to estimate the cross-spectra in TOPS mode [Pixel]. Default value: (2000, 3000 )

cd:Float.cd:Description: Shortest wavelength of output polar grid.
Longest wavelength of output polar grid.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Number of wavenumber bins in polar grid.
Number of directional bins in polar grid.

cd:Float.cd:Description: Parameters relative to velocity bunching.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Parameters relative to the calibration of the MTF to compensate for wind-dependent Hs Range Ratio. The parameter has an attribute named "beam" to allow decoupling the activation depending on the beam for WV (i.e: allowed attribute value WV1, WV2, S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6)

Parameters relative to the calibration of the MTF to compensate for wind-dependent Hs Eff Ratio. The parameter has an attribute named "beam" to allow decoupling the activation depending on the beam for WV (i.e: allowed attribute value WV1, WV2, S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6)

cd:Float.cd:Description: Value of the contrast between two partitions below which they should be merged during the removal of the low frequency noise (non-wave signatures).
Factor applied to the contrast between two partitions below which they should be merged during the swell spectrum partitioning.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Decide or not to merge partitions during the swell spectrum partitioning for which the spectral distance between the integral parameters is above the variable merge_close_thr (row below).

cd:Float.cd:Description: Factor applied to the contrast between two partitions below which they should be merged during the swell spectrum partitioning.
During the partitioning process, value for which swell spectrum values are not considered to present any relevant peak for the detection of the local peak.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Maximal number of partitions to be retrieved from the wave spectrum. The default value is: npart=5

cd:Float.cd:Description: Effective range resolution used to tune the RAR MTF. The default value: L_res=20
The lowest wavenumber used in the low frequency filtering process. α_h=2π/800
Resampling growth rate for cartesian spectrum compression from linear to sinh grid. The default value is: resGrowthRate=32
The half width of the lowpass filter for cartesian cross spectra resampling. The default value is 1
This factor is used to adjust the MTF, with a default value of 1100
Signal-to-noise ratio threshold for ambiguity removal. The default value is: γ_amb=0.02
Parameter used to tune the MTF for low wavenumbers. The default value is: α_mtf=0.25
Factors specifying the spectral clutter estimation region (range/azimuth sizes, azimuth position). The default values are: cultreg=[0.15, 0.04, 0.9]
Scaling lambda to give the ROI in azimuth. The default value is: njy=6.911504

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Allow activating the computation of the total significant wave height, value=True to activate the calculation, otherwise False
Allow activating the computation of the peak direction from Group analysis, value=True to activate the calculation, otherwise False
Allow to trigger the denoising on OSW

cd:Float.cd:Description: Threshold on percentage of Sea Coverage. Imagettes having a percentage of Sea Coverage below this threshold will not be processed with a full OSW inversion. Variables not generated will contain fill values. Default is 0%.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: If true, the model inference is performed using the models provided in AUX_ML2 auxiliary product, and if a failure is occurring, the LOP process stops with error. If false, the model inference is performed using the models provided in AUX_ML2 auxiliary product, however, if a failure is occurring, the LOP continues the processing either replacing the result of inference by legacy inversion process (if existing), or by using fill values. The model it applies to can be selected by the "for" attribute, with allowed values "TotalHS" and "Quality Flag"
Use the wind direction from ECMWF. The default value is set to True.

cd:Text.cd:Description: "legacy_empirical": Uses an empirical algorithm that has been implemented since the start of the mission (for IPF versions earlier than 4.0). "deep_learning": Uses a deep learning model to estimate the Hs wind sea (available for IPF versions 4.0 and later). "None": No Hs wind sea computation is performed for the product.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Use bathymetry data. The default value is set to True.
Use land mask data. The default value is set to True..
Estimate the Cross-spectra estimation from TOPS mode. The default value is set to False.

cd:Float.cd:Description: Size of the SAR derived wind field in the range direction. Wind cells should typically be square therefore nominally rangeCellSize = azimuthCellSize.
Size of the SAR derived wind field in the azimuth direction. Wind cells should typically be square therefore nominally azimuthCellSize = rangeCellSize.
Distance to shore where the processing is not performed.
Standard deviation error of the wind speed provided by ancillary wind information.
Standard deviation error of the wind direction provided by ancillary wind information.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Index or name of the Geophysical Model Function (GMF) to be used for the wind inversion, used for either for VV or HH polarisation data. Options supported are integers in the range [ 0, 21]: 0=gmf_cmod2_i3_nn, 1= gmf_cmod_ifr2_nn, 2=gmf_cmod_ifr2_bc_nn,3=gmf_cmod4_nn, 4=gmf_cmod2_i3, 5=gmf_cmod_ifr2, 6=gmf_cmod_ifr2_bc, 7=gmf_cmod4, 8=gmf_cmod5, 9=gmf_cmodcross2pol, 10=gmf_cmodrs2cross4pol, 11=gmf_cmodrs2cross4pol_vachon, 12=gmf_cmod5n, 13=gmf_cmod5h, 14=gmf_cmod5na, 15=gmf_cmod6, 16=gmf_sarmodVV, 17=gmf_sarmodHH, 18=gmf_cmodcross_h14e, 19=gmf_cmodcross_h14s, 20=gmf_cmodcross_ms1a, 21=gmf_cmodrs2cross2pol. The concerned polarisation is discriminated by the attribute named "polarisation" with supported value 'VV' or 'HH'

cd:Text.cd:Description: Name of the Geophysical Model Function (GMF) to be used for the wind inversion, used for either for VV or HH polarisation data. One GMF per polarisation (VV and HH) described as an attribute. Allowed values are cmod2_i3_nn, cmod_ifr2_nn, cmod_ifr2_bc_nn, cmod4_nn, cmod2_i3, cmod_ifr2, mod_ifr2_bc, mod4, mod5, modcross2pol, cmodrs2cross4pol, cmodrs2cross4pol_vachon, cmod5n, cmod5h, cmod5na, cmod6, sarmodVV, sarmodHH, cmodcross_h14e, cmodcross_h14s, cmodcross_ms1a, cmodrs2cross2pol
Name of the polarization ratio to be used for the wind inversion. It is considered only for HH polarisation. Allowed values are mouch1_ and zhang_2.

cd:Float.cd:Description: Value above which minimization in the inversion is considered low quality. Valid value range is between 0 and 1e29.
Value above which the calibration of the product is considered to be incorrect. Valid value range is between 0 and 10 dB.
Value above which the NRCS estimated at the SAR wind cell resolution is considered as low quality. Valid value range is between -30 and 10 dB.
Probability of false alarm for the removal of bright target.

cd:Integer.cd:Description: Allow activating the noise correction, value=True to activate the noise correction, otherwise False.

Allow the activation of bright target computation in a given channel 'polarisation', if the bright target computation is not activated for the given channel, it is not taking into account for combined bright target mask.

cd:Text.cd:Description: Parameter allowing to describe for a given channel, the origin of the Bright target mask that has been applied. Possible value examples: "None" No bright target mask applied, "VV" mask from bright target detected on VV only, "VV+VH" mask from bright targets detected on VV channel OR VH channel, "VVxVH" mask from bright targets detected on both VV channel AND VH channel.

cd:Record.cd:Description: Parameters threshold applied on RFI report annotation value to report the corresponding burst with RFI. Thresholds are expressed with the attribute beam, supported value ‘IW1’, ‘IW2’, ‘IW3’, ‘EW1’, ‘EW2’, ‘EW3’, ‘EW4’, ‘EW5’

cd:Float.cd:Description: Threshold on time Domain Percentage Affected Lines
Threshold on time Domain Avg Percentage Affected Samples
Threshold on time Domain Max Percentage Affected Samples
Threshold on freqDomainPercentageAffectedLines
Threshold on freqDomainMaxPercentageAffectedBw
Size of the Doppler estimation block in the range direction.
Size of the Doppler estimation block in the azimuth direction.
Size of grid cell interval in range direction.
Size of grid cell interval in azimuth direction
Factor of resolution reduction in the range dimension
Factor of resolution reduction in the azimuth dimension
Parameter used to compute the antenna coefficients
Size of the tile along the azimuth dimension for reading buffered data
Hanning window size along the azimuth dimension
Hanning window size along the range dimension

cd:Record.cd:Description: Parameters threshold applied on RFI report annotation value to report the corresponding burst with RFI. Thresholds are expressed with the attribute beam, supported value ‘IW1’, ‘IW2’, ‘IW3’, ‘EW1’, ‘EW2’, ‘EW3’, ‘EW4’, ‘EW5’

cd:Float.cd:Description: Threshold on time Domain Percentage Affected Lines
Threshold on time Domain Avg Percentage Affected Samples
Threshold on time Domain Max Percentage Affected Samples
Threshold on freqDomainPercentageAffectedLines
Threshold on freqDomainMaxPercentageAffectedBw
